name: rac-vscode
description: Inline validation for RAC (requirements-as-code) artifacts, powered by the rac CLI.

commands.title: RAC: What did we decide about…?
RAC: Enable Claude Code pre-edit hook
RAC: Disable Claude Code pre-edit hook

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with RAC
walkthroughs.description: Set up a requirements-as-code corpus and watch the editor enforce your recorded decisions.

steps.title: Install the rac CLI
steps.description:
The extension runs your local `rac` CLI and reimplements nothing. Install it with pipx, or point the extension at an existing install.
[Install with pipx](command:rac.installRac)
[Set rac.path](command:workbench.action.openSettings?%5B%22rac.path%22%5D)

steps.title: Set up your corpus
steps.description:
Create a `.rac/config.yaml` identity and a starter artifact via `rac quickstart`.
[Set Up Workspace](command:rac.setupWorkspace)

steps.title: See enforcement in your editor
steps.description: In a relationship section (e.g. `## Related Decisions`), reference a decision that does not exist — RAC flags it at the reference site, exactly as `rac relationships --validate` does on the CLI. That is the whole idea: the decisions your team already made, enforced where you type.

rac.path.markdownDescription: Path to the `rac` binary. Empty = the `RAC_BIN` environment variable, else `rac` on `PATH`.

rac.validate.enable.description: Validate RAC artifacts and show diagnostics as you open and save them.

scripts.vscode:prepublish: npm run check-types && npm run compile
scripts.pretest: npm run compile-tests && npm run compile
